briefdescription.para: The input event is originated from the manual API call.
The input event is from touch event.
The input event is from key event.
The input event is from tap gesture.
The input event is from long press gesture.
The input event is from wheel event.
Defines the type of input events.
Trigger click when the key is released.
Trigger click immediately when the key is pressed.
Disables click events from key inputs.
Defines the policy for when a click event is triggered via key input. This policy is specifically for D-Pad, Remote, or Keyboard interactions.
Managed by the parent container's layout (default).
Excluded from parent's layout accumulation; uses SetPositionX/SetPositionY.

detaileddescription.para: Default: the parent container's layout system manages this View's size and position (measure/arrange follow the parent's layout rules and the View participates in the parent's accumulation, spacing and index calculations).

Standalone: the parent container excludes this View from its accumulation/spacing/ index calculations. The View's size is still measured normally (so MATCH_PARENT, WRAP_CONTENT and explicit RequestedWidth/Height work as usual), but its position is taken from the View's own SetPositionX/SetPositionY (RequestedPositionX/Y) instead of being decided by the parent's layout. Useful for floating overlays, drag previews, tooltips and absolute positioning within an arbitrary LayoutManager.

briefdescription.para: Position is proportional to parent size.
Size is proportional to parent size.
Enumeration for identifying layout parameter types.

detaileddescription.para: Used by View::GetLayoutParams<T>() and View::SetLayoutParams() to look up the correct layout parameters attached to a View.

briefdescription.para: Show scroll bar only when scrolling.
Scroll to make the view visible.
Allow over-scrolling only when content is scrollable.
Enumeration for the start and end property ranges.
Represents current state of a control.
AccessibilityRole represents the purpose of a control.
Move keyboard focus towards the left direction.
Move keyboard focus towards the right direction.
Move keyboard focus towards the up direction.
Move keyboard focus towards the down direction.
Move keyboard focus towards the previous page direction.
Move keyboard focus towards the next page direction.
Move keyboard focus towards the forward direction.
Move keyboard focus towards the backward direction.
Move keyboard focus towards the clockwise direction.
Move keyboard focus towards the counter clockwise direction.
A regular keyboard, numberpad or attached buttons.
A mouse, trackball or touchpad relative motion device.
A touchscreen with fingers or stylus.
A pointing device based on laser, infrared or similar technology.
Set to API call, not device.
Relative to the view size (percentage [0.0, 0.5] of the shorter side)
Specifies how corner radius values are interpreted.

simplesect.para: Enum values match Ui::Visual::Transform::Policy::Type internally.

briefdescription.para: Also reorder layout children to match the new sibling order.
Keep layout children order unchanged; only visual z-order is affected.
Controls whether sibling-order changes (Raise/Lower/RaiseAbove/LowerBelow) also reorder the layout children list.

detaileddescription.para: Visual z-order (Actor sibling order) and layout order are kept in sync by default. Use PRESERVE when only the drawing/hit order should change while layout arrangement stays the same.

briefdescription.para: Enumeration for the find-text options (can be combined with bitwise OR).
Enumeration for the start and end property ranges.

memberdef.definition: using Dali::Ui::MeasureCallback = typedef Callback<MeasuredSize(View, float, float)>
memberdef.name: MeasureCallback

briefdescription.para: Move-only callback for custom layout measurement.

simplesect.para: Signature: MeasuredSize(View view, float widthConstraint, float heightConstraint)

memberdef.definition: using Dali::Ui::ArrangeCallback = typedef Callback<MeasuredSize(View, const LayoutRect&)>
memberdef.name: ArrangeCallback

briefdescription.para: Move-only callback for custom layout arrangement.

simplesect.para: Signature: MeasuredSize(View view, const LayoutRect& bounds)

memberdef.definition: using Dali::Ui::ColorOverrideFunc = typedef bool (*)(StringView colorId, Vector4& outColor)
memberdef.name: ColorOverrideFunc

briefdescription.para: Function pointer type for overriding theme color lookups.

detaileddescription.para: When registered via SetColorOverride(), this function is called before querying the theme loader for every color lookup. If the function returns true, the output color is used and the theme loader is skipped. If it returns false, the normal theme lookup proceeds.

parameterdescription.para: The color identifier being looked up
The overridden RGBA value (only used when returning true)

simplesect.para: true if the color is overridden, false to fall through to the theme

briefdescription.para: Typed callback for applying a resolved color to a View.

detaileddescription.para: Pass to UpdateBinding() to register a binding between a UiColor and a View.

memberdef.definition: using Dali::Ui::ExecutionKeyPredicate = typedef bool (*)(const Dali::String& keyName)
memberdef.name: ExecutionKeyPredicate

briefdescription.para: Function pointer type for determining whether a key triggers click execution.

simplesect.para: True if the key should trigger click execution

memberdef.definition: using Dali::Ui::StringGetterDelegate = typedef void (*)(void* obj, const char* propertyName, std::string* result)
memberdef.name: StringGetterDelegate

detaileddescription.para: When RequestedWidth or RequestedHeight is set to this value (the default), the View sizes to fit its content (natural size or children bounding box).
When RequestedWidth or RequestedHeight is set to this value, the View fills the parent container's available space on that axis.

briefdescription.para: Converts an integer spx value to a float pixel value.

detaileddescription.para: The input value is multiplied by a scaling-factor configured via UiConfig.

simplesect.para: UiConfigManager::Init() must have been called before using this operator. An assertion failure is triggered if UiConfig has not been initialized.

Do NOT use this operator to initialize static or global variables. Static initialization order is undefined across translation units, so UiConfigManager::Init() may not have been called yet at that point. Use lazy initialization or compute values at first access instead.
The calculated physical pixels as a float.

briefdescription.para: Converts a floating-point spx value to a float pixel value.

detaileddescription.para: The input value is multiplied by a scaling-factor configured via UiConfig.

simplesect.para: UiConfigManager::Init() must have been called before using this operator. An assertion failure is triggered if UiConfig has not been initialized.

Do NOT use this operator to initialize static or global variables. Static initialization order is undefined across translation units, so UiConfigManager::Init() may not have been called yet at that point. Use lazy initialization or compute values at first access instead.
The calculated physical pixels as a float.

briefdescription.para: Converts an integer dp value to a float pixel value.

detaileddescription.para: The input value is computed with dpi and baseline-dpi configured via UiConfig.

simplesect.para: UiConfigManager::Init() must have been called before using this operator. An assertion failure is triggered if UiConfig has not been initialized.

Do NOT use this operator to initialize static or global variables. Static initialization order is undefined across translation units, so UiConfigManager::Init() may not have been called yet at that point. Use lazy initialization or compute values at first access instead.
The calculated physical pixels as a float.

briefdescription.para: Converts a floating-point dp value to a float pixel value.

detaileddescription.para: The input value is computed with dpi and baseline-dpi configured via UiConfig.

simplesect.para: UiConfigManager::Init() must have been called before using this operator. An assertion failure is triggered if UiConfig has not been initialized.

Do NOT use this operator to initialize static or global variables. Static initialization order is undefined across translation units, so UiConfigManager::Init() may not have been called yet at that point. Use lazy initialization or compute values at first access instead.
The calculated physical pixels as a float.

briefdescription.para: Converts an integer sdp value to a float pixel value.

detaileddescription.para: The input value is computed with dpi, baseline-dpi and scaling-factor configured via UiConfig.

simplesect.para: UiConfigManager::Init() must have been called before using this operator. An assertion failure is triggered if UiConfig has not been initialized.

Do NOT use this operator to initialize static or global variables. Static initialization order is undefined across translation units, so UiConfigManager::Init() may not have been called yet at that point. Use lazy initialization or compute values at first access instead.
The calculated physical pixels as a float.

briefdescription.para: Converts a floating-point sdp value to a float pixel value.

detaileddescription.para: The input value is computed with dpi, baseline-dpi and scaling-factor configured via UiConfig.

simplesect.para: UiConfigManager::Init() must have been called before using this operator. An assertion failure is triggered if UiConfig has not been initialized.

Do NOT use this operator to initialize static or global variables. Static initialization order is undefined across translation units, so UiConfigManager::Init() may not have been called yet at that point. Use lazy initialization or compute values at first access instead.
The calculated physical pixels as a float.

briefdescription.para: Creates a new Alpha discard effect.

detaileddescription.para: Alpha discard effect is used to discard fragments when the alpha colour value is below a threshold. This is useful for stenciling.
Usage example:
ImageView actor = ImageView::New( EXAMPLE_IMAGE_PATH ); Property::Map alphaDiscardEffect = CreateAlphaDiscardEffect(); actor.SetProperty( ImageView::Property::IMAGE, alphaDiscardEffect );

simplesect.para: A property map of the required shaders.

memberdef.argsstring: (Actor &actor, const Vector2 &position, const Vector2 &displacement, float initialProgress)
memberdef.name: DissolveEffectSetCentralLine

detaileddescription.para:
Use one point (position) and one direction ( displacement ) vector to define this line As we use the texture coordinate as pixel position to calculate random offset, the line should passing through rectangle {(0,0),(0,1),(1,0),(1,1)}, so make the position parameter with two component values between 0.0 to 1.0
actor
The actor that registers the uniform properties
position
The point ( locates within rectangle {(0,0),(0,1),(1,0),(1,1)} ) passed through by the central line
displacement
The direction of the central line
initialProgress
The normalised initial progress of the shader
DissolveEffect is a custom shader effect to achieve Dissolve effects in image views.
Animatable/Constrainable uniforms: "uPercentage" - This value is proportional to the distortion applied; a value of zero means no distortion.

parameterdescription.para: True if using high precision in fragment shader for fully random noise, false otherwise

simplesect.para: The newly created Property::Map with the dissolve effect

detaileddescription.para: Creates a new DistanceFieldEffect
DistanceFieldEffect is a custom shader effect to achieve distance field on Image actors
Animatable/Constrainable uniforms - These will need to be registered to the actor as a custom property to take into effect:
"uDoGlow" - The glow state. If true, glow is enabled "uGlowBoundary" - The glow boundary factor "uGlowColor" - The glow color multiplier
"uDoShadow" - The shadow state. If true, shadows is enabled. Cannot be used with glow/and or outline "uShadowColor" - The shadow color multiplier "uShadowOffset" - The shadow offset

"uDoOutline" - The outline state. If true, outline is enabled "uOutlineColor" - The outline color multiplier "uOutlineParams"- Thickness of outline. The outline thickness is determined by two values. First value [0-1] Specifies the distance field value for the center of the outline. Second value [0-1] Specifies the softness/width/anti-aliasing of the outlines inner edge.

simplesect.para: The newly created Property::Map with the distance field effect

detaileddescription.para: ImageRegionEffect is a custom shader effect to show only a region of an Image actor.

Animatable/Constrainable uniforms: "uTopLeft" - The top-left corner of the image region. The coordinates are in percentage, (0,0) being the top-left and (1,1) the bottom right of the original image "uBottomRight" - The bottom-right corner of the image region. The coordinates are in percentage, (0,0) being the top-left and (1,1) the bottom right of the original image

simplesect.para: A property map of the required shader

briefdescription.para: Set the properties for the motion blur.

parameterdescription.para: The actor that registers the uniform properties
Number of samples used by the shader

detaileddescription.para: Motion blur shader works on a per object basis. Objects will blur when they move, or if the camera moves.

Animatable/Constrainable uniforms: "uBlurTexCoordScale" - This scales the offset for texture samples along the motion velocity vector. A smaller value means the samples will be spaced closer, larger value further apart. User should use this to get the blur to look contiguous, i.e. the blur texels should not be too widely spread, with gaps in between. Default 0.125. "uGeometryStretchFactor" - This scales the amount the geometry stretches backwards along the motion velocity vector. A smaller value means the geometry stretches less, larger it stretches more. User should use this to get the blur to 'bleed' into areas outside the physical bounds of the actor. We need this as the blur is only applied inside the bounds of the actor, but you would expect motion blur trails where the actor was previously but is there no longer. Default 0.05. "uSpeedScalingFactor" - This takes the magnitude of the motion velocity vector and scales it to produce a value which is used to fade the blur in / out with the speed that the actor is moving. As the blur fades in, more of the blur is visible and less of the original actor, and viceversa. This value is also used to control how much to fade the actor near the edges, based on the speed the actor is moving. When the actor is at rest this is not applied. Default 0.5. "uObjectFadeStart" - The displacement from the centre of the actor that the actor will start to fade towards its edges. This is used to prevent an unsightly hard edge between the blurred actor and the scene. Depends on the values of the vertices in the vertex stream. When the actor is at rest this is not applied. Default 0.25, which is half way towards the edge for an ImageVisual::QUAD. "uObjectFadeEnd" - The displacement from the centre of the actor that the actor will finish fading towards its edges. This is used to prevent an unsightly hard edge between the blurred actor and the scene. Depends on the values of the vertices in the vertex stream. When the actor is at rest this is not applied.Default 0.5, which is all the way towards the edge for an ImageVisual::QUAD. "uAlphaScale" - Global scaler applied to the alpha of the actor. Used to make the blurred actor a bit more subtle (helps to hide discontinuities due to limited number of texture samples) and reveal a bit of the background behind it as it moves. When the actor is at rest this is not applied. Default 0.75. "uNumSamples" - The number of texture samples to be taken. Increasing the number of samples provides better quality at the cost of performance. "uModelLastFrame" - The model to world space transformation matrix of the actor in the previous frame.

simplesect.para: The newly created Property::Map with the motion blur effect

briefdescription.para: Set the properties for the motion stretch.

detaileddescription.para: Motion stretch shader works on a per object basis. Objects will stretch in the direction of motion when they move, or if the camera moves.

Animatable/Constrainable uniforms: "uGeometryStretchFactor" - This scales the amount the geometry stretches along the motion velocity vector. A smaller value means the geometry stretches less, larger it stretches more. Default 0.5. "uSpeedScalingFactor" - This value is used to control how much to fade the actor near the edges, based on the speed the actor is moving. When the actor is at rest this is not applied. Default 0.5. "uObjectFadeStart" - The displacement from the centre of the actor that the actor will start to fade towards its edges. This is used to prevent an unsightly hard edge between the stretched actor and the scene. Depends on the values of the vertices in the vertex stream. When the actor is at rest this is not applied. Default Vector2(0.25, 0.25), which is half way towards the edge for an ImageVisual::QUAD. "uObjectFadeEnd" - The displacement from the centre of the actor that the actor will finish fading towards its edges. This is used to prevent an unsightly hard edge between the stretched actor and the scene. Depends on the values of the vertices in the vertex stream. When the actor is at rest this is not applied. Default 0.5, which is all the way towards the edge for an ImageVisual::QUAD. "uAlphaScale" - Global scaler applied to the alpha of the actor. Used to make the stretched actor a bit more subtle and reveal a bit of the background behind it as it moves. When the actor is at rest this is not applied. Default 0.75. "uModelLastFrame" - The model to world space transformation matrix of the actor in the previous frame.

simplesect.para: The newly created Property::Map with the motion stretch effect

briefdescription.para: Retrieves the UiConfigImpl from a UiConfig handle.

simplesect.para: A reference to the internal implementation

briefdescription.para: Retrieves the UiConfigImpl from a const UiConfig handle.

simplesect.para: A const reference to the internal implementation

briefdescription.para: Retrieves the UiConfigManagerImpl from a UiConfigManager handle.
Retrieves the UiConfigManagerImpl from a const UiConfigManager handle.
Retrieves the UiThemeManagerImpl from a UiThemeManager handle.
Retrieves the UiThemeManagerImpl from a const UiThemeManager handle.
Retrieves the UiComponentConfigImpl from a UiComponentConfig handle.

simplesect.para: A reference to the internal implementation

briefdescription.para: Retrieves the UiComponentConfigImpl from a const UiComponentConfig handle.

simplesect.para: A const reference to the internal implementation
